requirement.original: Analyze and specify the fix for three bypass paths in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py identified by Codex review of cycle 20260522-080646.
requirement.clarified: Fix three specific code paths in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py where strip_non_executable_contexts() fails to recognise a shell executable context, causing dangerous tokens to pass through the context-stripping step without detection. Add regression tests. Do not rewrite the module.
requirement.what: Patch _process_shell_interp (combined flags), _find_command_word (wrapper option arguments), and _process_heredocs (absolute path pipe-to-shell).
requirement.why: The safety hook pretool-bash-safety.sh relies on COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED produced by bash_context_strip.py. When the stripper misclassifies an executable shell context as a quoted-string context, danger-token grep rules never see the dangerous token, allowing commands like 'bash -lc rm ...' or 'sudo -u root bash -c rm ...' to bypass the block.

included: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py — production logic changes at lines 298, 219-222, 353
hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py — regression tests for all 10 ACs
excluded: Any other file in the repository
Module rewrite or refactor beyond the three targeted code paths
Wrapper-option arity for env, timeout, nice, ionice, nohup (deferred)

success_criteria: AC1: bash -lc 'rm /tmp/x' exposes rm as executable
AC2: bash -ce 'rm /tmp/x' exposes rm as executable
AC3: sudo -u root bash -c 'rm /tmp/x' exposes rm
AC4: sudo -H bash -c 'rm /tmp/x' exposes rm (no over-skip)
AC8: | /tmp/bash.old heredoc body NOT preserved (non-shell basename)
AC9: all existing tests in test_bash_safety_context.py pass
constraints: Must not introduce new exception paths (fail-closed invariant)
Must not over-skip wrapper arguments in BUG 2 fix
BUG 3 fix must use _SHELL_INTERPS membership check via _basename(), not a broader regex

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Wrapper-option arity for env -i, timeout <n>, nice -n <n>, ionice -c <n> is also absent. These wrappers can similarly shift the command word. Not in scope for this cycle (BUG 2 fix targets sudo only).

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Commands using combined shell flags (bash -lc), wrapper option pairs (sudo -u root bash), or absolute-path shells in heredoc pipes (| /bin/bash) are not detected as executable shell contexts by strip_non_executable_contexts(), so the dangerous tokens inside their payloads are silently dropped rather than preserved for grep detection.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Three independent exact-match / regex gaps in bash_context_strip.py: (1) _process_shell_interp uses text.strip() == '-c' which cannot match any combined flag form; (2) _find_command_word skips one token per wrapper prefix regardless of whether that prefix consumes option arguments, so any flag-value pair following the wrapper shifts the command word index; (3) _process_heredocs uses a literal-name regex for pipe-to-shell that does not match absolute path forms.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — not a regression; these are pre-existing precision gaps in the initial implementation of bash_context_strip.py discovered by Codex xhigh review.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The module was written as a conservative, non-full-parser bounded classifier. The three code paths each made a simplifying assumption (exact token match, single-token wrapper skip, literal-name regex) that holds for the common case but fails for real-world combined-flag and absolute-path shell forms.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The gaps are exploitable by any subagent that constructs a shell command using standard combined-flag or absolute-path syntax, bypassing all four grep-based danger-token rules in pretool-bash-safety.sh.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Pre-existing since the module was introduced. High-recurrence per Codex item A classification.
observations: Codex exec (baseline f79fba1c) confirmed: strip_non_executable_contexts("bash -lc 'rm /tmp/x'") returns 'bash -lc \'rm /tmp/x\'' — rm hidden inside quoted token (observed)
Codex exec confirmed: strip_non_executable_contexts("sudo -u root bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'") returns 'sudo -u root bash -c ' — payload stripped (observed)
Codex exec confirmed: strip_non_executable_contexts("cat <<'EOF' | /bin/bash\nrm /tmp/x\nEOF") returns heredoc body as empty line — rm invisible (observed)
Codex exec confirmed: bash -lc, bash -ce, bash -cx, dash -cl, dash -ce all exit 0 — combined flags are standard POSIX (observed at runtime)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (has runtime-verified observations from Codex exec session)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Codex exec ran the python module directly against the three bypass input forms and captured the raw output. Each output confirmed the payload was not expanded. Line numbers confirmed by reading the file.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression — gaps are pre-existing in original implementation; no prior working state to bisect to

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Combined flags: bash -lc 'rm ...' or bash -ec 'kill ...' where -lc contains -c but does not equal -c
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: strip_non_executable_contexts('bash -lc \'rm /tmp/x\'') exposes rm as executable text

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: sudo -u root bash -c '...' — the -u root shifts the position so bash is not recognized as the shell interpreter
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: strip_non_executable_contexts('sudo -u root bash -c \'rm /tmp/x\'') exposes rm as executable text

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: curl ... | bash does NOT match absolute paths like | /bin/bash or | /usr/bin/bash
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: heredoc body in 'cat <<\'EOF\' | /bin/bash\nrm /tmp/x\nEOF' is preserved as executable text
All existing tests in hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py continue to pass

component_chain.role: Calls bash_context_strip.py via CMD_INPUT env var and captures COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; runs danger-token grep rules against stripped output
component_chain.evidence: Lines 726-731 read via Read tool: 'if [ -r "$HOOKS_DIR_CTX/lib/bash_context_strip.py" ]; then CMD_INPUT="$COMMAND" timeout ... "$PYTHON_BIN" "$HOOKS_DIR_CTX/lib/bash_context_strip.py"'

component_chain.role: Implements strip_non_executable_contexts(); the three bugs are in _process_shell_interp (line 298), _find_command_word (lines 219-222), _process_heredocs (line 353)

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Fail-closed guard: oversized input returns raw command unchanged to prevent unbounded processing
Fail-closed guard: any exception returns raw command unchanged so the hook always has something to check
Defensive advancement invariant in _split_top_level: prevents infinite loops when a scanner branch fails to advance

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths. This is a pre-tool-use hook executed inside the harness before any user-facing action.

evidence.observed: Codex review (cycle 20260522-080646, item A): three bypass patterns in bash_context_strip.py cause dangerous tokens to pass through context stripping undetected.

measured.value: BUG1: text.strip() == '-c' at line 298; BUG2: idx += 1; continue at lines 219-222 with no option-arg skip; BUG3: r'\|\s*(bash|sh|zsh|dash)\b' at line 353

expected.value: BUG1: detect 'c' anywhere in a short-option cluster; BUG2: consume sudo option-argument pairs before resuming command-word search; BUG3: compare _basename() of piped word to _SHELL_INTERPS
expected.source: POSIX.1-2017 shell invocation spec; Codex exec runtime verification; _SHELL_INTERPS set at bash_context_strip.py:21

evidence.gap: BUG1: all combined-flag forms (-lc, -ec, -ce, -xc, +c, +ec) bypass detection. BUG2: any wrapper followed by >=1 flag-value pair bypasses shell-interp recognition. BUG3: any absolute-path shell in a pipe heredoc bypasses detection.

reference_source.description: POSIX.1-2017 Shell Command Language, invocation section; Codex exec runtime verification of combined-flag shell invocations

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt; first cycle addressing these three bypass paths.

context.recent_commits: f79fba1 auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for master

development_approach.strategy: Surgical patch of three specific conditions in bash_context_strip.py. Each bug is a one-to-three line change. Add 8-10 pytest test cases to test_bash_safety_context.py covering all ACs. No structural refactor.
development_approach.validation_approach: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v; all existing tests must pass; new tests for AC1-AC10 must pass.

_risk_level_doc: Security-relevant hook fix. The file is executed on every Bash tool call. A regression that widens false-negatives silently reduces safety coverage; a regression that widens false-positives can block legitimate operations. Both failure modes are high-impact.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 5 findings, all classified as BLOCKER. Key corrections applied: (1) BUG 1 fix direction updated from 'c must be last in cluster' to 'c anywhere in cluster, including +c/+ec forms'; (2) BUG 2 fix scoped to explicit sudo arity table rather than generic option-skipping; (3) scope updated to include test file; (4) BUG 3 fix implementation specified as _first_word()+_SHELL_INTERPS rather than loose regex; (5) ACs are fully enumerated with concrete pass/fail examples.

findings.issue: BUG 1 fix too narrow: 'ends with c' misses -ce, -cx, and +c forms
findings.proposed_fix: Detect c anywhere in short-option cluster; include +c/+ec forms; exclude -- long options
findings.rationale: Codex exec verified -ce, -cx, +c all accepted by bash/dash at runtime. Fix direction updated to 'c in text.strip()[1:]' predicate.

findings.issue: BUG 2 fix under-specified: 'skip any option-like tokens' can over-skip
findings.proposed_fix: Use explicit sudo arity table (_SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG) covering -u/--user/-g/--group/-C/-D, with -H (no-arg) and -- (terminator) handled separately
findings.rationale: Codex is correct that -H takes no argument. Over-skip would cause -H's next token (bash) to be consumed as an argument, breaking detection. AC4 and AC5 added to spec.
Security-relevant fix without regression tests is unacceptable.

findings.issue: BUG 3 regex too broad: .*bash could match /tmp/bash.old
findings.proposed_fix: Use _first_word()+_SHELL_INTERPS rather than extended regex; AC8 added as negative test
findings.rationale: Architecturally cleaner and reuses proven code path.

findings.issue: ACs were absent from the draft summary
findings.proposed_fix: Add concrete pass/fail cases for all bypass forms
findings.rationale: ACs AC1-AC10 fully specified in ticket.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 blockers addressed. BUG 1 predicate widened; BUG 2 sudo arity table specified; test file added to scope; BUG 3 implementation uses _first_word()+_SHELL_INTERPS; ACs AC1-AC10 written.
